results.description: All cloud resources MUST carry mandatory cost allocation tags: cost-center, owner, environment, and workload. Operate Phase – Tagging and Cost Attribution
Cloud Cost Governance Policy – Resource Ownership

results.description: Cost budgets MUST be defined as IaC resources (not manually in cloud console UIs) for each cloud account, subscription, or project. Budget alerts MUST be configured at 80% and 100% of the budget limit and automatically routed to the workload owner and FinOps team. Budget resources in IaC ensure they are version-controlled, reviewable, and reproducible.

controls: Inform Phase – Budget Visibility and Accountability
Operate Phase – Budget Management and Variance Reporting
Cloud Cost Governance Policy – Budget Enforcement

results.description: All persistent compute resources (EC2 instances, VMs, RDS instances, Kubernetes node groups) MUST carry a 'rightsizing-reviewed' tag with the date of the last review (ISO 8601, YYYY-MM-DD). Reviews must be performed at least every 90 days. Resources tagged with a review date older than 90 days are considered non-compliant. Idle detection must be configured for all compute workloads (CPU utilization < 5% over 7 days triggers an alert).

controls: Optimize Phase – Rightsizing and Resource Optimization
Cloud Cost Governance Policy – Resource Efficiency

check_results.check_title: EC2 instances must have 'rightsizing-reviewed' tag
check_results.message: EC2 instance is missing the 'rightsizing-reviewed' tag. All compute resources must be actively reviewed.
check_results.remediation: Add 'rightsizing-reviewed' tag to all EC2 instances and launch templates. Value format: YYYY-MM-DD (ISO 8601 date of last review). Example: rightsizing-reviewed = "2025-03-01" Schedule the next review within 90 days.

results.description: All storage resources (S3 buckets, Azure Storage accounts, GCS buckets) MUST have explicit lifecycle rules configured. All log groups (CloudWatch, Azure Log Analytics, GCP Cloud Logging) MUST have an explicit retention_in_days value that is not 0 (infinite) and not null. No storage or logging resource may be provisioned without an explicit, finite retention or lifecycle policy. Infinite retention is classified as Architectural Cost Debt.

controls: Optimize Phase – Storage Optimization and Lifecycle Management
Art. 5(1)(e) – Storage limitation (data not kept longer than necessary)
Art. 17 – Right to erasure (lifecycle policies support GDPR deletion requirements)
Data Retention Policy – Explicit lifecycle for all data categories
Cloud Cost Governance – No infinite retention defaults

check_results.check_title: S3 buckets must have explicit lifecycle configuration
check_results.message: All assertions passed.
check_results.remediation: Add an aws_s3_bucket_lifecycle_configuration resource for every S3 bucket. Include at minimum: transition to STANDARD_IA after 30 days, GLACIER after 90 days, and an abort_incomplete_multipart_upload rule with days_after_initiation = 3.

results.control_title: Cost Impact Assessment in Architecture Decision Records
results.description: Every Architecture Decision Record (ADR) covering infrastructure changes MUST include a structured Cost Impact Assessment section. The assessment MUST cover: TCO estimate (EUR/year), lock-in risk score (1-5), data transfer cost estimate, operational effort (FTE/year), exit cost estimate, and 3-year NPV. ADRs with lock-in risk score >= 4 require Architecture Board approval before implementation. This control is the primary prevention mechanism for Architectural Cost Debt.

controls: Operate Phase – Architecture and Cost Governance
FinOps Principles – Everyone Takes Ownership of Their Cloud Usage
Cloud Cost Governance Policy – Lock-in Assessment Requirement

check_results.check_title: High lock-in resources must be tagged with lock-in risk score
check_results.message: All assertions passed.
check_results.remediation: Add 'lock-in-risk' tag (value: 1-5) to document that vendor lock-in was consciously evaluated. Tag value 4 or 5 requires an ADR with Architecture Board approval. See docs/adr/ for the ADR template with Cost Impact Assessment section.

check_results.check_title: High lock-in resources must be tagged with lock-in risk score
check_results.message: High lock-in resource must have 'lock-in-risk' tag (value: 1-5) documenting conscious evaluation.
check_results.remediation: Add 'lock-in-risk' tag (value: 1-5) to document that vendor lock-in was consciously evaluated. Tag value 4 or 5 requires an ADR with Architecture Board approval. See docs/adr/ for the ADR template with Cost Impact Assessment section.

results.description: Monthly engineering-level FinOps reviews MUST take place for all production workloads. Quarterly Architecture Board reviews covering cost debt and strategic cost decisions MUST occur. Review outputs (action items with owner and due date) MUST be documented in writing and accessible in the team's task tracking system. Reviews are mandatory, not optional – absence of reviews is a control failure.

controls: Operate Phase – Review Cadence and Continuous Improvement
FinOps Principles – Teams Need Timely and Accurate Data
Cloud Cost Governance Policy – Mandatory Review Cadence
Architecture Governance – Quarterly Cost Debt Review

check_results.check_title: Workload resources should have current 'last-finops-review' tag
check_results.message: Resource is missing 'last-finops-review' tag. Monthly FinOps reviews required.
check_results.remediation: After each monthly FinOps review, update the 'last-finops-review' tag on workload resources to the current date. This can be automated in CI as part of the review process. Value format: YYYY-MM-DD.

results.description: Logging and observability resources MUST be organized in explicit retention tiers: Hot (7-30 days), Warm (30-90 days), Cold (90-365 days), Archive (>365 days). CloudWatch Log Groups MUST NOT have retention_in_days = 0 (infinite). Operational log groups MUST have retention_in_days <= 365. DEBUG-level logging in production is prohibited without Architecture Board justification. High-volume trace sampling MUST be configured for services with >1000 req/s.

controls: Art. 5(1)(e) – Storage limitation (logs containing personal data must not be kept indefinitely)
Cloud Cost Governance – Observability Cost Target < 20% of Total

results.description: Production deployments MUST use safe deployment patterns – canary releases, blue/green deployments, or feature flags – to reduce blast radius and enable fast rollback. results.description: All production infrastructure MUST be continuously compared against its IaC definition. Emergency manual changes MUST be codified in IaC within 24 hours.

results.description: All known operational debt – manual processes, undocumented workarounds, toil-generating tasks, outdated runbooks, siloed knowledge – MUST be tracked in a version-controlled Operational Debt Register. results.description: All stateless compute workloads handling variable or unpredictable traffic MUST have auto-scaling configured with appropriate minimum, desired, and maximum capacity thresholds. Auto-scaling policies MUST be based on meaningful metrics (CPU utilization, request latency, queue depth, or custom application metrics), not just time-based schedules. Auto-scaling configurations MUST be tested under realistic load conditions before production deployment and re-validated after significant architectural changes.

check_results.check_title: AWS Auto Scaling Groups must have scaling policies defined
check_results.message: No matching resources found in Terraform configuration.
check_results.remediation: Add scaling policies to the Auto Scaling Group. At minimum, add target tracking scaling based on ALBRequestCountPerTarget or CPU utilization. Set min_size >= 1, max_size >= 2. Configure health checks to use ELB type for web applications.

check_results.check_title: Azure VMSS must have autoscale settings configured
check_results.message: No matching resources found in Terraform configuration.
check_results.remediation: Enable autoscale settings for the VMSS. Configure at least one profile with scale-out and scale-in rules based on CPU percentage or custom metrics.

check_results.check_title: GCP Managed Instance Groups must have autoscaler configured
check_results.message: No matching resources found in Terraform configuration.
check_results.remediation: Add a google_compute_autoscaler resource targeting the Managed Instance Group. Configure autoscaling_policy with appropriate min_replicas, max_replicas, and cpu_utilization target or load_balancing_utilization.

results.description: All workloads with repeated read patterns or high-latency data sources MUST have a caching strategy defined and implemented. The caching strategy MUST specify cache layers (application, distributed cache, CDN), TTL policies, cache invalidation mechanisms, and cache hit rate targets. CDN caching MUST be configured for all public-facing static and dynamic content. Distributed caching solutions (Redis, Memcached, or equivalents) MUST be used for session data and frequently-accessed read-heavy datasets.

controls: PERF 3 – How do you select your storage solution?

check_results.check_title: AWS ElastiCache clusters must be in cluster mode with automatic failover
check_results.message: ElastiCache replication group must have automatic_failover_enabled = true for production use.; ElastiCache must have at least 2 cache nodes for redundancy.; ElastiCache must have at-rest encryption enabled.
check_results.remediation: Set automatic_failover_enabled = true, num_cache_clusters >= 2, and at_rest_encryption_enabled = true. For Redis, use replication_group resource with at least one replica.

check_results.check_title: Azure Cache for Redis must be Premium or Standard tier with persistence
check_results.message: No matching resources found in Terraform configuration.
check_results.remediation: Upgrade Redis SKU to Standard or Premium. Disable non-SSL port. Set minimum_tls_version = "1.2". For large datasets enable cluster mode (Premium only).

check_results.check_title: GCP Memorystore for Redis must use STANDARD_HA tier
check_results.message: No matching resources found in Terraform configuration.
check_results.remediation: Set tier = "STANDARD_HA" for production Redis instances. Set transit_encryption_mode = "SERVER_AUTHENTICATION" for encryption in transit.

results.description: All relational and document databases used in production MUST have a documented performance baseline including query execution times, I/O throughput, and connection pool utilization. An index strategy MUST be defined and maintained for all tables with significant read/write loads. Slow query logging MUST be enabled and reviewed regularly. Database performance insights or equivalent monitoring MUST be active on all production database instances. Missing indexes on frequently queried columns in high-traffic tables are treated as violations.

results.description: All production services MUST have Service Level Objectives (SLOs) defined for performance-relevant metrics: latency (P95 and P99), error rate, and availability. Alerting MUST be configured to notify on-call teams when SLO burn rate exceeds acceptable thresholds. SLOs MUST be reviewed and updated quarterly or after major architectural changes.

Where serverless is appropriate, Lambda, Cloud Functions, Azure Functions, or managed container services (Fargate, Cloud Run) MUST be configured with appropriate memory, timeout, concurrency, and cold-start mitigation strategies. Serverless function configurations MUST be optimized to avoid under-memory allocation, excessive timeouts, and uncontrolled concurrency.

check_results.check_title: AWS Lambda functions must have explicit memory and timeout configuration
check_results.message: Lambda memory_size must be explicitly set >= 256MB for production functions (default 128MB is rarely optimal).; Lambda timeout must be explicitly set (not default 3s) and sized appropriately.; Lambda reserved_concurrent_executions should be configured to control account concurrency usage.
check_results.remediation: Run AWS Lambda Power Tuning to find optimal memory allocation. Set timeout = 2x measured P99 execution time. Set reserved_concurrent_executions to limit blast radius on account concurrency.

results.description: All block storage (EBS, Azure Managed Disks, GCP Persistent Disk) and object storage used by production workloads MUST be configured with appropriate I/O performance settings (IOPS, throughput, storage class). check_results.check_title: AWS EBS volumes must use gp3 type instead of gp2
check_results.message: EBS volume must not use gp2 type. Migrate to gp3 for consistent performance and lower cost.; EBS volume type must be explicitly set to a supported current type.
check_results.remediation: Change volume_type from "gp2" to "gp3". For databases requiring higher IOPS, set explicit iops and throughput values on gp3 (max 16000 IOPS, 1000 MB/s). Migration from gp2 to gp3 is online and non-disruptive.

results.description: All production services MUST expose health check endpoints and configure readiness and liveness probes. Load balancers and orchestrators MUST use health checks to route traffic only to healthy instances. Kubernetes workloads MUST define readinessProbe and livenessProbe. AWS load balancers MUST have health check paths configured. Health check intervals, thresholds, and timeout values MUST be explicitly defined – no reliance on cloud provider defaults.

check_results.message: No matching resources found in Terraform configuration.
check_results.remediation: Add a health_check block to the aws_lb_target_group resource. Define path, interval, timeout, healthy_threshold, and unhealthy_threshold explicitly.

check_results.message: No matching resources found in Terraform configuration.

results.description: All production stateful and stateless workloads MUST be deployed across a minimum of two Availability Zones (AZs). Databases, caches, and message queues MUST use multi-AZ configurations with automatic failover. Load balancers MUST span at least two AZs. Auto-scaling groups and Kubernetes clusters MUST distribute nodes and pods across AZs. Single-AZ deployments in production are not acceptable without explicit documented risk acceptance.

controls: REL 10 – Use fault isolation to protect your workload

check_results.check_title: AWS RDS instances must have Multi-AZ enabled
check_results.message: All assertions passed.
check_results.remediation: Set multi_az = true on the aws_db_instance resource. This creates a synchronous standby replica in a different AZ. Failover is automatic and typically completes within 1-2 minutes.

check_results.check_title: AWS Auto Scaling Group must span at least 2 availability zones
check_results.message: No matching resources found in Terraform configuration.
check_results.remediation: Set vpc_zone_identifier to a list containing subnet IDs from at least 2 AZs. Increase min_size to 2 to ensure at least one instance per AZ during failures.

check_results.check_title: Azure database services must use zone-redundant configuration
check_results.message: No matching resources found in Terraform configuration.
check_results.remediation: Add a high_availability block with mode = "ZoneRedundant" and configure standby_availability_zone to a different zone than the primary.

check_results.check_title: GCP Cloud SQL must use REGIONAL availability type
check_results.message: No matching resources found in Terraform configuration.
check_results.remediation: Set availability_type = "REGIONAL" in the settings block of google_sql_database_instance to enable automatic failover to a different zone.

results.description: All production databases, object stores, and stateful services MUST have automated backup configurations with defined retention periods. Backup configurations MUST specify RPO-aligned retention. Recovery procedures MUST be tested at minimum quarterly through documented restore exercises. Backups MUST be stored in a separate account, region, or vault from the primary data source. Point-in-time recovery (PITR) MUST be enabled for all production databases. Untested backups are not backups.

check_results.check_title: AWS RDS must have backup retention >= 7 days with PITR enabled
check_results.message: RDS backup retention must be at least 7 days for production.
check_results.remediation: Set backup_retention_period >= 7 on the aws_db_instance resource. Enable deletion_protection = true to prevent accidental deletion. Configure backup_window to a low-traffic maintenance window.

check_results.check_title: AWS S3 buckets with production data must have versioning enabled
check_results.message: S3 bucket versioning must be Enabled for production data buckets.
check_results.remediation: Add an aws_s3_bucket_versioning resource referencing the bucket with versioning_configuration { status = "Enabled" }.

check_results.check_title: Azure PostgreSQL Flexible Server must have backup retention >= 7 days
check_results.message: No matching resources found in Terraform configuration.
check_results.remediation: Set backup_retention_days >= 7 and geo_redundant_backup_enabled = true on the azurerm_postgresql_flexible_server resource.

check_results.check_title: GCP Cloud SQL must have automated backups and PITR enabled
check_results.message: No matching resources found in Terraform configuration.
check_results.remediation: Set enabled = true and point_in_time_recovery_enabled = true in the backup_configuration block of the google_sql_database_instance settings.

results.description: All inter-service and downstream HTTP/gRPC calls MUST define explicit timeout values. Critical service dependencies MUST implement circuit breaker patterns. Retry logic MUST use exponential backoff with jitter. Connection pools MUST have defined maximum sizes. Services MUST implement bulkhead isolation for different dependency classes. No service may rely on default (infinite or undefined) timeouts for external calls.

controls: REL 10 – Use fault isolation to protect your workload

check_results.check_title: AWS ALB idle timeout must be explicitly configured
check_results.message: No matching resources found in Terraform configuration.

check_results.check_title: Azure Application Gateway backend settings must define explicit timeouts
check_results.message: No matching resources found in Terraform configuration.
check_results.remediation: Set request_timeout_in_seconds in the backend_http_settings block. Recommended values: 30 for APIs, 120 for batch operations.

check_results.check_title: GCP Cloud Run services must define explicit request timeout

check_results.remediation: Set timeout_seconds in the template.spec block of google_cloud_run_service. Default is 300s; set to the appropriate value for your workload latency SLO.

results.description: Every production workload MUST have a documented Incident Response (IR) plan with defined severity levels, escalation paths, and on-call rotations. Runbooks MUST exist for all critical alerts and MUST be version-controlled. Runbooks MUST be linked directly from alert notifications. Ad-hoc or unsanctioned "accidental chaos" is not a substitute.

results.description: All AWS accounts MUST enforce MFA for all IAM users with console access. Root account usage MUST be prohibited for day-to-day operations. IAM password policy MUST meet minimum security requirements: minimum length of 14 characters, complexity requirements, password reuse prevention of at least 5 prior passwords, and maximum password age of 90 days. check_results.check_title: AWS IAM account password policy must meet minimum security requirements
check_results.message: IAM password policy must require a minimum of 14 characters.; IAM password policy must prevent reuse of at least 5 prior passwords.; IAM password policy must set maximum password age to 90 days or less.
check_results.remediation: Define an aws_iam_account_password_policy resource with all required attributes set. Ensure minimum_password_length >= 14, all require_* flags are true, password_reuse_prevention >= 5, and max_password_age <= 90.

results.description: IAM policies MUST NOT grant wildcard actions (Action: "*") combined with wildcard resources (Resource: "*"). The AWS-managed AdministratorAccess policy MUST NOT be attached to roles used for automated workloads, CI/CD pipelines, or application service accounts. All IAM permissions MUST follow the principle of least privilege, granting only the minimum rights required for the intended function. Privilege escalation paths MUST be identified and eliminated using IAM Access Analyzer.

results.control_title: Encryption at Rest with Customer-Managed Keys
results.description: All data stores containing sensitive or classified data MUST be encrypted at rest using customer-managed keys (CMK) stored in AWS KMS. Automatic key rotation MUST be enabled for all CMKs. Encryption using only provider-managed keys (SSE-S3/AES256) is NOT sufficient for sensitive, PII, financial, or regulated data. KMS key deletion windows MUST be set to a minimum of 7 days. KMS key policies MUST restrict access to explicitly authorized principals only.

controls: Art. 32 – Security of processing: encryption of personal data
Art. 25 – Data protection by design and by default

check_results.check_title: RDS instances and clusters must be encrypted at rest with a CMK
check_results.message: RDS instance/cluster must have storage encryption enabled (storage_encrypted = true).; RDS instance/cluster must use a customer-managed KMS key (kms_key_id must be set).
check_results.remediation: Set storage_encrypted = true and kms_key_id = aws_kms_key.rds_key.arn on the aws_db_instance or aws_rds_cluster resource. Note: enabling encryption on an existing unencrypted RDS instance requires creating an encrypted snapshot and restoring to a new instance. Plan for a maintenance window.

check_results.check_title: KMS keys must have automatic rotation enabled and a sufficient deletion window
check_results.message: KMS key must have automatic rotation enabled (enable_key_rotation = true).; KMS key deletion window must be at least 7 days to allow recovery from accidental deletion.
check_results.remediation: Set enable_key_rotation = true on all aws_kms_key resources. Set deletion_window_in_days to at least 7 (recommend 30 for production keys). Note: key rotation does not apply to asymmetric keys or keys in external key stores; those require separate rotation procedures.

results.description: All network communication between clients and services, and between internal services, MUST use TLS 1.2 or higher. HTTP listeners on application load balancers MUST either redirect to HTTPS or be prohibited. CloudFront distributions MUST enforce a minimum TLS policy of TLSv1.2. Internal service-to-service communication within VPCs MUST be encrypted. Expired, self-signed, or untrusted TLS certificates MUST NOT be used in production environments.

controls: Art. 32 – Security of processing: encryption of personal data in transit

check_results.check_title: ALB and NLB listeners must use HTTPS, not HTTP
check_results.message: No matching resources found in Terraform configuration.
check_results.remediation: Change the listener protocol to HTTPS and provide a valid ACM certificate ARN. If a port 80 listener is required for redirect purposes, change the default_action to type = "redirect" with redirect { protocol = "HTTPS", status_code = "HTTP_301" }.

check_results.check_title: CloudFront distributions must enforce TLS 1.2 or higher
check_results.message: No matching resources found in Terraform configuration.
check_results.remediation: Add a viewer_certificate block to the aws_cloudfront_distribution resource. Set minimum_protocol_version = "TLSv1.2_2021" and ssl_support_method = "sni-only". Use a custom ACM certificate instead of the default CloudFront certificate for production workloads with custom domains.

results.description: All compute resources MUST be placed in private subnets unless they explicitly serve public traffic. results.description: Backup policies MUST explicitly define region/location constraints, minimum retention periods, and restore-test schedules. Cross-region replication MUST be explicitly configured and documented with an approved sovereign destination. Backup storage MUST remain within the same jurisdictional boundary as primary data unless explicitly approved and documented.

controls: Art. 32 – Security of processing (availability and resilience)

results.description: For all data classes, the key ownership model MUST be explicitly defined and technically enforced: Provider-Managed Keys (PMK), Customer-Managed Keys (CMK), Bring Your Own Key (BYOK), or Hold Your Own Key (HYOK). Key rotation and deletion MUST be governed by documented procedures. All data at rest MUST be encrypted; encryption with default provider-managed keys requires explicit approval for sensitive data classes.

controls: Art. 32 – Security of processing (encryption as a technical measure)
Art. 17 – Right to erasure (cryptographic erasure via key deletion)

check_results.check_title: AWS KMS CMKs must have automatic key rotation enabled
check_results.message: KMS key must have enable_key_rotation = true.
check_results.remediation: Set enable_key_rotation = true on all aws_kms_key resources.

check_results.check_title: KMS key deletion window must be explicitly set (>= 14 days)
check_results.message: KMS key deletion_window_in_days must be >= 14 days.
check_results.remediation: Set deletion_window_in_days >= 14 on all KMS keys.

check_results.check_title: S3 buckets must use KMS (CMK) encryption, not AES256
check_results.message: S3 bucket must use 'aws:kms' encryption, not 'AES256' (provider-managed keys).; S3 KMS encryption must reference a specific CMK, not the default AWS managed key.

results.description: All compute resources MUST prefer energy-efficient processor architectures (ARM64/Graviton for AWS, Ampere Altra for Azure, T2A for GCP) over equivalent x86 instance families. AWS Lambda functions MUST use arm64 architecture unless the workload has a verified incompatibility. EC2 instances MUST NOT use previous-generation families (t2, m4, c4, r4) when current-generation equivalents are available. ARM/Graviton instances deliver 20-40% better performance-per-watt compared to equivalent x86 instances, reducing both cost and carbon emissions simultaneously.

check_results.remediation: Replace previous-generation instance types with current-generation equivalents: t2 -> t4g (Graviton2, 40% better price-performance), m4 -> m7g (Graviton3, best performance-per-watt), c4 -> c7g (Graviton3, compute-optimized), r4 -> r7g (Graviton3, memory-optimized). Test compatibility before migration using AWS Graviton migration guide.

check_results.check_title: Azure VMs should use Ampere Altra (Dpsv5/Epsv5) or equivalent efficient series
check_results.message: No matching resources found in Terraform configuration.
check_results.remediation: Migrate VMs to Dpsv5 (Ampere Altra ARM) for general workloads or Epsv5 for memory-intensive. For x86-only workloads, use Ddsv5 or Edsv5 as the most energy-efficient x86 option. Run Azure Migrate assessment to validate compatibility before resizing.

results.description: Workloads without strict data-residency requirements MUST consider carbon intensity of cloud regions in placement decisions. When architectural and regulatory constraints allow, workloads SHOULD be placed in regions with high renewable energy mix or low grid carbon intensity. Region selection decisions MUST document sustainability considerations alongside latency and cost factors. Batch workloads with flexible placement MUST evaluate green region alternatives.

controls: ESRS E1 – Climate Change – Scope 3 reduction actions
Scope 2 – Market-based method (renewable energy certificates, PPAs)
Scope 3 – Category 11: purchased cloud services location factor
6.1.2 – Environmental aspects: procurement and infrastructure decisions

check_results.check_title: AWS provider region should be a low-carbon-intensity region
check_results.message: AWS region has a high carbon intensity. Consider us-west-2, eu-north-1, eu-west-1 for lower emissions.
check_results.remediation: If workloads can be placed in a lower-carbon region without violating data residency, latency, or compliance requirements, prefer: us-west-2 (Oregon, ~89% renewable), eu-north-1 (Stockholm, ~95% renewable), eu-west-1 (Ireland, ~80% renewable). If high-carbon region is required, document the justification in an ADR and offset through RECs for that region's workload footprint.

results.description: All cloud compute resources MUST be continuously monitored for utilization. Resources with CPU utilization below 5% for 14 consecutive days MUST be flagged as idle and subject to a stop/terminate review. Non-production environments MUST have scheduled shutdown policies (evenings and weekends). Autoscaling groups MUST be configured for all stateless compute workloads to eliminate idle capacity during off-peak hours. EC2 instances with persistent idle state MUST be converted to spot instances, reserved capacity, or terminated.

controls: ESRS E1 – Climate Change – resource efficiency measures
Scope 3 Category 11 – reducing purchased cloud compute emissions

check_results.check_title: AWS autoscaling groups must have scale-in enabled to eliminate idle capacity
check_results.message: No matching resources found in Terraform configuration.
check_results.remediation: Configure autoscaling groups with target tracking scaling policies based on CPU or request count. Set min_size to 0 for non-production environments to allow complete scale-in during off hours. Use predictive scaling for production to balance performance and sustainability.

check_results.check_title: Non-production autoscaling groups should allow spot instances
check_results.message: No matching resources found in Terraform configuration.
check_results.remediation: Add mixed_instances_policy with spot allocation for non-production environments. For production: use 20-40% spot on top of on-demand base capacity. For dev/test: use 100% spot with instance type diversification for resilience.

check_results.check_title: GCP managed instance groups must have autoscaler configured
check_results.message: No matching resources found in Terraform configuration.
check_results.remediation: Configure google_compute_autoscaler with CPU utilization target of 60-70% and set min_replicas = 0 for non-production. For production, set min_replicas = 1 and max_replicas based on peak load testing.

results.description: All storage resources (S3 buckets, EBS volumes, Azure Blob Storage, GCS buckets) MUST have lifecycle policies defined. Data MUST be automatically tiered to cost- and energy-efficient cold storage after defined activity thresholds. Log data MUST have retention limits. Temporary data (build artifacts, test data, cache) MUST have explicit expiration. Unattached EBS volumes and unused snapshots MUST be detected and purged. Organizations MUST apply the principle of data minimization: store only what is needed, for as long as needed, in the most efficient tier.

controls: ESRS E1 – Climate Change – resource efficiency in IT operations
Article 25 – Data protection by design and by default
Scope 3 Category 11 – reducing storage-related cloud emissions
Hardware Efficiency – minimize data stored in high-energy tiers
Energy Efficiency – data tiering and lifecycle management

check_results.check_title: S3 buckets must have lifecycle configuration
check_results.message: All assertions passed.
check_results.remediation: Add lifecycle rules to all S3 buckets. Minimum recommended configuration: Transition to STANDARD_IA after 30 days, GLACIER after 90 days, expire after retention-policy-defined period. Enable S3 Intelligent-Tiering for unpredictable access pattern buckets as an alternative.

check_results.check_title: EBS volumes should be set to delete on instance termination
check_results.message: Root EBS volume should be set to delete on termination to prevent orphaned volumes.
check_results.remediation: Set delete_on_termination = true on root_block_device for EC2 instances unless you have an explicit requirement to retain root volumes. For data volumes, use separate aws_ebs_volume resources with lifecycle management.

check_results.check_title: Azure storage accounts should have blob lifecycle management policies
check_results.message: No matching resources found in Terraform configuration.
check_results.remediation: Add lifecycle management rules to Azure Storage accounts. Transition blobs to Cool tier after 30 days, Archive tier after 90 days. Configure delete rules based on data retention policy. Use azurerm_storage_management_policy resource.

results.description: Batch workloads, report generation, data processing pipelines, and other non-latency-sensitive jobs MUST be scheduled during off-peak hours (22:00–06:00 local grid time) when carbon intensity of the electricity grid is typically lower. Workloads with flexible execution windows SHOULD integrate carbon intensity APIs (WattTime, electricityMaps) to execute during low-carbon windows. All EventBridge scheduled jobs and cron-based workloads MUST document their scheduling rationale including whether carbon-aware timing was considered.

controls: Software Carbon Intensity (SCI) – time-based carbon optimization
ESRS E1 – Climate Change – operational energy optimization
Scope 2 – Market-based method – time-of-use carbon intensity

check_results.check_title: EventBridge Scheduler schedules should use flexible time windows for batch workloads
check_results.message: No matching resources found in Terraform configuration.
check_results.remediation: Set flexible_time_window { mode = "FLEXIBLE"; maximum_window_in_minutes = 120 } on all non-latency-sensitive batch schedules. This allows AWS to execute the job within a 2-hour window, enabling carbon-aware timing without missing SLAs.

check_results.check_title: AWS Batch compute environments should use SPOT allocation strategy
check_results.message: No matching resources found in Terraform configuration.
check_results.remediation: Change compute_resources.type to "SPOT" for AWS Batch compute environments. Configure spot_iam_fleet_role and bid_percentage = 100 to maximize spot usage. Implement retry policies on batch jobs to handle spot interruptions gracefully.

check_results.check_title: GCP Cloud Scheduler jobs should use UTC timezone and off-peak scheduling
check_results.message: No matching resources found in Terraform configuration.
check_results.remediation: Set time_zone = "Etc/UTC" on all Cloud Scheduler jobs and adjust schedule expressions to target off-peak UTC hours (22:00-06:00 UTC). This ensures consistency with carbon intensity data which is typically published in UTC.

results.description: Software MUST be designed with energy efficiency as an explicit quality attribute. Architecture Decision Records MUST include energy efficiency considerations for algorithm and data structure choices. Dependencies MUST be reviewed for efficiency: unused libraries, heavy frameworks, and polling patterns MUST be replaced with lightweight alternatives and event-driven designs. Software Carbon Intensity (SCI) SHOULD be measured for critical workloads and included in architecture reviews. Teams MUST avoid over-engineering: monolithic frameworks, excessive microservice proliferation, and redundant compute layers all increase energy consumption without delivering proportional value.

controls: Software Carbon Intensity (SCI) – measurement and optimization standard
Energy Efficiency – algorithmic and architectural efficiency
Carbon Efficiency – reducing energy per functional unit
ESRS E1 – Climate Change – operational measures to reduce emissions
6.1.2 – Environmental Aspects – software design as environmental aspect

check_results.check_title: Lambda functions should not attach excessive numbers of layers
check_results.message: No layers attribute found – this check passes; single-package deployments are preferred.
check_results.remediation: Review Lambda layer usage. Consolidate multiple utility layers into a single optimized layer. Use dependency management (requirements.txt, package.json) to control package size. Consider Lambda container images for complex dependency trees with multi-arch builds.

check_results.check_title: SQS event source mapping should use long polling for Lambda triggers
check_results.message: No matching resources found in Terraform configuration.
check_results.remediation: Set maximum_batching_window_in_seconds = 30 (or higher) and batch_size = 100 on SQS event source mappings to maximize message batching. This reduces Lambda invocations by up to 90% for high-volume queues, directly reducing energy per message processed.

check_results.check_title: GCP Cloud Run services should scale to zero in non-production
check_results.message: No matching resources found in Terraform configuration.
check_results.remediation: Set min_instance_count = 0 for all non-production Cloud Run services. For production services with strict cold-start SLAs, use min_instance_count = 1 and document the sustainability trade-off in the architecture decision record.

results.description: All data transfers MUST be designed to minimize unnecessary movement across network boundaries. Cross-region data transfers MUST be justified; same-region communication MUST be preferred. Content Delivery Networks (CDN) MUST be used for all user-facing static and semi-static content. HTTP compression (gzip/brotli) MUST be enabled for all API responses larger than 1KB. VPC Endpoints MUST be used for AWS service communication to avoid internet gateway egress. Data transfer optimization is both a cost control and a carbon reduction measure: every byte transferred consumes network energy; minimizing transfers minimizes emissions.

controls: Energy Efficiency – minimize network data transfer
Software Carbon Intensity (SCI) – network embodied energy
ESRS E1 – Climate Change – operational energy efficiency
Scope 3 Category 11 – reducing network-related cloud emissions

check_results.check_title: CloudFront distributions must enable compression
check_results.message: No matching resources found in Terraform configuration.
check_results.remediation: Set compress = true on all cache_behavior and default_cache_behavior blocks in the CloudFront distribution. CloudFront will automatically compress eligible responses (text/html, text/css, application/json, etc.) using gzip or brotli.

check_results.check_title: VPC should have S3 and DynamoDB gateway endpoints to avoid internet egress
check_results.message: All assertions passed.
check_results.remediation: Create gateway VPC endpoints for com.amazonaws.REGION.s3 and com.amazonaws.REGION.dynamodb in each VPC. Associate them with all route tables. For interface endpoints (STS, ECR, CloudWatch, SSM): create them in private subnets to eliminate all internet gateway dependencies.

check_results.check_title: Azure CDN endpoints must have compression enabled
check_results.message: No matching resources found in Terraform configuration.
check_results.remediation: Set is_compression_enabled = true and provide content_types_to_compress list including text/html, text/css, application/json, application/javascript. Ensure origin also supports compressed responses or configure CDN-level compression.

results.description: Organizations subject to CSRD (or voluntarily reporting ESG) MUST have documented processes for collecting, aggregating, and reporting cloud IT emissions as part of Scope 3 GHG inventory. Cloud IT emissions data MUST be exported from cloud provider tools at least quarterly. CSRD-scope organizations MUST map cloud emissions to ESRS E1-6 reporting requirements. Emission reduction targets MUST be documented and tracked against baseline. Where possible, ESG data collection MUST be automated (scheduled exports, API integrations) to ensure data quality and reduce manual reporting burden.

controls: ESRS E1-6 – Gross Scope 1, 2, and 3 GHG emissions and total GHG emissions
ESRS 2 – General Disclosures: data collection and reporting processes
Scope 2 Guidance – market-based and location-based methods
ICT Sector Guidance – target setting and tracking
Near-term corporate targets – annual progress reporting

check_results.check_title: S3 bucket for Cost Explorer / Carbon Footprint Tool exports must exist with appropriate retention
check_results.message: ESG/sustainability S3 buckets should have a 'purpose' tag for audit identification.
check_results.remediation: Create a dedicated S3 bucket for sustainability data exports. Tag it with purpose = "esg-reporting" and configure lifecycle policies for 7-year retention (CSRD requires 7 years for sustainability disclosures). Enable versioning and access logging for audit trail completeness.

results.description: Organizations MUST maintain a Sustainability Debt Register documenting all known gaps between current sustainability posture and target state across all WAF-SUS controls. The register MUST be reviewed and updated at least quarterly. Each entry MUST include: estimated carbon impact, remediation priority, owner, and target resolution date. Sustainability debt MUST be treated as a governance risk item and reported in the organization's ESG or sustainability report. Quarterly reviews MUST produce measurable progress against reduction targets.

controls: ESRS 2 – General Disclosures: Governance, strategy and management of sustainability matters
ESRS E1 – Climate Change: governance of climate-related risks and opportunities
Corporate Standard – management and governance of GHG inventory
ICT Sector Guidance – progress tracking and governance
6.1 – Actions to address risks and opportunities

check_results.check_title: Compute resources should have sustainability-reviewed tag to indicate review date
check_results.message: Resource has no sustainability-reviewed tag – add date of last sustainability review.
check_results.remediation: Add sustainability-reviewed = "YYYY-MM-DD" tag to all major compute resources. Set up quarterly reminder to update this tag after sustainability review. Use AWS Config custom rule to alert when sustainability-reviewed tag is older than 180 days.
